mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in > 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% indicates restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based demand strength that lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score interaction of RSAFS and HOUST produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recoveries, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals rebounding capacity utilization and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO increases are not positive in > 60% of instances during early-cycle periods over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Three-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) signal contracting consumer spending and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.0% signals labor market distress that reduces household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) reduce real earnings expectations and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not negative in > 55% of months with rising CPIAUCSL over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and real GDP (GDPC1) signals a synchronized expansion in supply and demand that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and GDPC1 produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
